seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Moa Island and Moffat Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Moa Island vs Moffat Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Moa Island and Moffat Beach.
heading: Moa Island vs Moffat Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Moa Island, Queensland and Moffat Beach, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Moa Island has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Moffat Beach (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Moa Island or Moffat Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Moa Island has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Moffat Beach scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Moa Island scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Moffat Beach scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Moa Island or Moffat Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Moa Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
